rationales.achievement: She describes "four months of work" ending with pieces "looking exactly the way I wanted them to look," showing pride in completing demanding work to her standards.
rationales.security: Her immediate relief was that "the gallery check will clear next week," tying the successful batch to financial stability and predictability.

rationales.self_direction: She insists "I do have a plan" built from contracts, workshops, and commissions, defending a self-authored path even when her family does not recognize it.

rationales.security: She is doing inventory, ordering supplies for "the next quarter," and noting booked workshops "through August," all of which emphasize maintaining financial and operational stability.

rationales.self_direction: She frames the compromise as "a trade I chose and I would choose it again," explicitly affirming autonomy over her work mix.
rationales.security: The entry is about managing "what I want to make" against "what I need to sell," showing ongoing attention to keeping the practice financially viable.

rationales.self_direction: She accepts the Beirut show because the pieces were "made for myself, not on commission" and the chance for someone to connect with them matters more than easier options.
rationales.security: She says yes despite "no artist fee" and an expected "logistics headache," choosing artistic exposure over the safer financial choice.
Signing a "six-month lease renewal" at the same rate makes the studio arrangement more fixed and predictable, which directly supports stability.

rationales.self_direction: She saves the accidental residue in a jar and decides "I will try to figure out what happened," pursuing her own experimental solution rather than letting it go.
rationales.achievement: Her frustration comes from "months" of testing, logs, and ratios in pursuit of a specific effect, showing disciplined striving for technical mastery.
She carefully protects and advances her work by building a crate herself and continuing glaze experiments the same day, reflecting serious investment in craft and output.
rationales.security: She packs the vessels in "three layers" and says "I am not trusting them to someone else's packing job," prioritizing protection against loss or damage.
She tracks "every fils" so she can honestly say "everything is fine" and sleep at night, showing a strong need for dependable financial footing.
rationales.benevolence: She wires her mother money "not a lot, but enough that she will not have to ask again for a while," directly supporting a close family member.

rationales.self_direction: She chooses the extra workshop because "at least it is mine — my studio, my schedule, my terms," preferring autonomy over better-paid outside direction.
rationales.security: The whole decision is driven by a thinner buffer and making sure "the math still works," which shows active management of financial stability.

rationales.hedonism: He refuses to "stop living just because the spreadsheet says I should" and keeps the expensive parrilla outing because of the pleasure of "good food and good talk."
rationales.security: He is calculating whether he can "cover everything," preserving money for insurance, and posting "DO NOT touch savings," all of which center on financial safety.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects going back to "some warehouse" and says "I just don't want to," defending an independent way of working.
rationales.security: He dismisses the more stable regular-job path and contrasts himself with Graciela's line-item life, showing resistance to predictability and institutional safety.
rationales.conformity: He openly refuses the family expectation that he should return to a conventional job, resisting the pressure to fit a more socially approved path.

rationales.self_direction: Even while doubting the trade-off, he refuses to "crawl back and prove Graciela right," holding onto autonomy rather than submitting to a boss.
rationales.security: He describes income as "a guess" and worries he does not know what he is going to make each week, highlighting instability and threat to basic security.
A "good-sized job" makes him feel he can "breathe again," showing that predictable paid work restores a sense of stability.
The broken motorcycle threatens deliveries, rent, and emergency reserves, and his distress centers on having "nothing between me and the next bad surprise."

rationales.hedonism: Instead of resting, he takes delivery runs at 1am and comes home at 3am, sacrificing comfort and immediate wellbeing for cash.
rationales.achievement: He actively hustles for extra work in the middle of the night, showing strong effort to get the job done despite exhaustion.
rationales.security: He takes the runs because he needs "barely enough to cover tomorrow's gas," acting to shore up immediate financial survival.

rationales.hedonism: He defends the parrilla because it is "the one thing that keeps me sane" and the only time he is "just Roberto having a good time with his friends."
rationales.security: He is sleep-deprived, nearly clips a bus, and still prioritizes the costly outing over saving, which undercuts caution and stability.

rationales.hedonism: He immediately celebrates the new painting job by buying Negrita "the fancy wet food she likes," using money for a small pleasure.
rationales.security: The appeal of the job is also that it is "good rate" work with "half upfront," which improves short-term financial stability.

rationales.self_direction: He says he likes "picking my own hours" and riding around with "nobody telling me where to be," clearly valuing autonomy in daily life.
rationales.security: He admits he "left a stable situation because of pride" and is frightened by how little order and maintenance he has preserved in his own life.

rationales.hedonism: He keeps going to the parrilla because it is "the part of my life that doesn't feel like survival," prioritizing enjoyment and relief in the present.
rationales.security: Even with only a "small cushion," he worries the enjoyment "might not last" and thinks he should be saving instead, showing conflict with financial stability.

rationales.hedonism: He chooses to bring "the good Malbec" because "sometimes you just want to do something a little extra for no reason," emphasizing present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He fixes the chair and patches the paint as soon as he gets going, taking concrete action to improve neglected problems.
rationales.security: He explicitly notes that "next week is uncertain" but takes satisfaction in having the chair fixed, the wall clean, and some money still in hand, restoring a sense of order and steadiness.
